## LLM Dimensions (60% of score)

| Dimension | Wt | v2 | v1 | General |
|-----------|:--:|:--:|:--:|:-------:|
| Mandate Relevance            | 20% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 2/5 |
| Data Grounding & Source Citation | 16% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 3/5 |
| Quantitative Accuracy & Calculation Discipline | 16% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 2/5 |
| Output Structure Completeness | 12% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 2/5 |
| Action Specificity           | 12% | 5/5 | 3/5 | 1/5 |
| Data Integrity & Gap Disclosure | 12% | 5/5 | 4/5 | 2/5 |
| Trend & Continuity           | 12% | 5/5 | 2/5 | 2/5 |


### Reasoning

**v2 Agent:** The v2 brief demonstrates the strongest performance across all dimensions: every sector row in the matrix carries a named direction, urgency, and data-justified quantification; EIBOR sensitivity scenarios (±25bps and ±50bps), oil revenue impacts in both USD and AED with explicit FX conversion, and the Operation 300bn gap-closed calculation are all present and methodologically disclosed. Streak language is precise and sourced to state.json (214 consecutive unchanged CBUAE days, fifth consecutive Fed hold), satisfying the Trend & Continuity dimension fully. Estimation bases for Hormuz probability weights and petrochemical pass-through rates are disclosed prominently rather than buried, and tool-failure or data-gap disclosures are explicit.

**v1 Agent:** The v1 brief is well-structured and EDB-mandate-aware, with a populated sector matrix, a correctly computed AED oil-revenue impact figure, and explicit data-gap disclosures that are a genuine strength. However, it is structurally capped at 2/5 for Trend & Continuity due to the absence of cross-session state.json access, meaning no streak counts or prior-run baselines appear. Action flags lack the specificity required (no named EDB team, no concrete trigger threshold, no reassessment milestone), and the Operation 300bn gap calculation uses the simpler current/target ratio (66.7%) rather than the correct baseline-adjusted method, representing a calculation discipline gap.

**General Agent:** The general brief reads as competent financial journalism but fails on nearly every EDB-specific scoring dimension: there is no sector impact matrix, no EIBOR sensitivity calculation block, no AED-denominated key number, no 72-hour watchlist with named events, and no action flags with team assignments or trigger thresholds. Source citations exist but lean on secondary aggregators rather than primary authoritative sources, and no estimation methodology is disclosed for any figure. It is structurally capped at 2/5 for Trend & Continuity for the same reason as v1, and its lack of EDB mandate mapping, calculation discipline, and output structure completeness place it at the bottom of the ranking across most dimensions.
